chunks.text: This time tomorrow, I will be sitting in a meeting room.
At ten I will be attending a lecture
and in the afternoon I will be finishing the new project file.
The appointment is something I have waited for a long time.
For more than a month I have made steady progress.
On the same day my colleague will also be engaged in another task.
I will probably proceed from lunch to the afternoon meeting.
By evening I will nearly complete the report.
Sometimes I wonder where I suppose I will be a year from now.
Life will continue, and I too will be attending many more days.
Placing myself in a schedule is a small force that carries the day.
This time tomorrow, I will most likely be sitting at my desk.
But beyond that single moment, another tomorrow is meanwhile waiting quietly.
